commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ca2e0b1244fe77c18312089cb636dc8a06d9d920)
commands.review_summary:
The PR appends native exec approval setup guidance to agent-side plugin approval no-route/timeout failures and adds focused before_tool_call coverage for Telegram-originated approvals.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +140. Total +175 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main returns generic plugin approval no-route/timeout text in `requestPluginToolApproval`, and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95800 includes v2026.6.9 timeout logs; I did not run a live Telegram gateway in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a patch that keeps exec setup guidance tied to the plugin failure cause; automation cannot satisfy the external proof gate.

target.title: fix(telegram): explain disabled plugin approval failures
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes #95800.

Telegram-originated plugin approvals, such as `skill_workshop apply`, could fail with a generic no-route or timeout message when Telegram native approvals were disabled because `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom` were not configured. The Telegram setup guidance already existed, but the agent-facing approval failure did not surface it.

## Why This Change Was Made
The approval hook already receives the turn source. When a plugin approval fails with no route or times out, it now resolves the initiating native exec approval surface for that channel/account. If native exec approval delivery is disabled and the channel provides setup text, the blocked tool reason appends that guidance instead of only saying no route or timeout.

The plugin approval surface and exec approval surface are resolved independently, so a plugin-only route failure does not produce misleading exec setup instructions.

This keeps the current approver-gating model intact and does not auto-route or enable Telegram approvals.

## User Impact
Telegram-only operators get an actionable explanation telling them to use Web UI or terminal UI for now, or configure `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` / `commands.ownerAllowFrom`, instead of a silent timeout-shaped denial.

## Evidence
- Behavior proof, focused on the reported approval path: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/exec-approvals.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts` passed 4 Vitest shards, 169 tests.
- The new agent approval coverage asserts both failed routes include the Telegram setup guidance when native exec delivery is disabled:
  - no-route: `Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)` plus `Approve it from the Web UI or terminal UI for now... Configure channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers or commands.ownerAllowFrom.`
  - timeout: `Approval timed out` plus the same Telegram setup guidance.
- Regression coverage also asserts a plugin-disabled / exec-enabled route failure stays generic and does not append exec setup text.
- Terminal proof from the `runBeforeToolCallHook` approval path printed the after-fix blocked-tool reasons:

```text
NO_ROUTE_REASON:
Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)

Approve it from the Web UI or terminal UI for now. Telegram supports native exec approvals for this account. Configure `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` or `commands.ownerAllowFrom`; leave `channels.telegram.execApprovals.enabled` unset/`auto` or set it to `true`.

TIMEOUT_REASON:
Approval timed out

Approve it from the Web UI or terminal UI for now. Telegram supports native exec approvals for this account. Configure `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` or `commands.ownerAllowFrom`; leave `channels.telegram.execApprovals.enabled` unset/`auto` or set it to `true`.
```

- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof run `28051331964` attempted real Telegram Desktop proof but did not capture usable visual evidence. Its manifest says the approval-failure chat path could not be reproduced in the available capture environment, so it produced no PR proof artifact.
- Autoreview was attempted after the follow-up patch but blocked before review by local Codex config: `Error loading config.toml: unknown variant priority, expected fast or flex in service_tier`.

## Notes
- The first push to `openclaw/openclaw` failed with GitHub 403, so this PR is from the `MonkeyLeeT/openclaw` fork with maintainer edits enabled.
- Issue assignment to `@steipete` also failed from this token with GitHub 403.
- Local Crabbox proof could not start from this machine because AWS credentials were unavailable: `failed to refresh cached credentials, no EC2 IMDS role found`.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
